text.title: Internal Linking Graph Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in solo internal-link audits. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix keyed to a methodology rule.

antipattern.symptom: Audit shows 11 orphans; founder publishes the graph anyway because "they get search traffic, so they're fine".
antipattern.root-cause: Orphan = no inbound INTERNAL link; the page leaks equity even with external referrals.
antipattern.fix: Add ≥1 inbound link from a sibling spoke OR fold the page into the closest cluster OR de-index. Validator rejects audit with orphans non-empty. Rule r4.

antipattern.symptom: Hub URL ranks position 14 for the head term; site has 22 internal links to that URL all using the exact head-term anchor.
antipattern.root-cause: Founder copied-pasted the same anchor across every link insertion.
antipattern.fix: Diversify to ≥3 surface forms per target (head term, partial match, descriptive sentence, brand+term). Rule r5.

antipattern.symptom: Hub page has 87 inbound links but only 6 outbound spokes; PageRank pools at the hub without flowing back to the cluster.
antipattern.root-cause: Founder treats hub as the cluster's destination, not the gateway.
antipattern.fix: Add explicit outbound links from hub to each spoke in the cluster overview section. Confirm bidirectional flow. Rule r2 + r6.

antipattern.symptom: Cluster hub ranks well but spokes underperform; spokes have inbound from sibling spokes but none from the hub itself.
antipattern.root-cause: Hub copy treats spokes as "see also" footers (chrome) instead of in-body links.
antipattern.fix: Move spoke references into the hub body as in-body links. Footer modules do not satisfy r3. Rule r3 + r6.
